reuseCandidates.signature: function runCappedPool({ items, concurrency, worker, now?, onEvent? }): Promise<{ results, summary }>
reuseCandidates.reuse: Concurrency-capped parallel pool primitive with never-throw settlement and ordered results. Generalizes council dispatchSeats with K-in-flight cap. Inject worker pattern.
reuseCandidates.notes: Pure pool primitive — worker is injected. The `xargs -P K` shape. settleItem never throws; mirrors council settleSeat.

reuseCandidates.signature: function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission): { command, args, input?, env }
reuseCandidates.notes: Each executor kind has its own CLI flag shape. codex uses stdin (- prompt), gemini uses argv, claude uses -p, opencode uses run + prompt arg, devin uses --print + stdin. This is the per-executor command builder a new loop type would need to extend.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function handleCodeGraphQuery(args: QueryArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
reuseCandidates.notes: Handles outline/calls_from/calls_to/imports_from/imports_to/blast_radius. Calls ensureCodeGraphReady before read. Reuse: call directly for programmatic graph queries without MCP transport.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function ensureCodeGraphReady(rootDir: string, options?: EnsureReadyOptions): Promise<ReadyResult>
reuseCandidates.notes: Auto-triggers selective/full reindex before reads. Returns ReadyResult with freshness/action/reason. Every read handler calls this. Reuse: gate any code-graph-dependent operation.

reuseCandidates.signature: Standalone code-graph package with CocoIndex decoupled, 10 sub-themes, 90% complete
reuseCandidates.notes: The most complete track. Key surfaces: runtime-and-scan (004), resilience-and-advisor (005), extraction-and-isolation (006), docs-and-readmes (007), playbook-validation (010). Code graph is a structural surface distinct from memory store. CocoIndex decoupled at 95% (002-deprecate-coco-index).

reuseCandidates.signature: 8-track phase-parent coordination surface with validate.sh handoff contract
reuseCandidates.notes: The 8-track decomposition (000-008) and phase transition rules are the canonical program structure. Any future program-packet can adopt this topology. Phase handoff criteria at spec.md:117-123 define cross-track dependency contracts.

reuseCandidates.signature: Memory quality, continuity gates, memory-save rewrite, runtime hardening, indexer invariants
reuseCandidates.notes: 001-continuity-memory-runtime is complete. The memory-save rewrite and continuity gates are the live subsystem. Cross-links to code-graph (distinct structural surface, not a shared store).

reuseCandidates.signature: async function evaluateCouncilConvergence(ns, options?): convergenceResult
reuseCandidates.notes: Thresholds: agreementRatio≥0.67, dissentDensity≤0.25, evidenceDepth≥1.0, unresolvedCritical=0, decisionConfidence≥0.65. bridgePayload() flattens to standard graph_decision/graph_signals shape.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function dispatchCouncilSeats({ roundId, seats, dispatchSeat, context?, now? }): Promise<{ round_id, started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, results[], summary }>
reuseCandidates.notes: settleSeat never throws (mirrors settleItem pattern). Seats are normalized to {id, input}. Now/settleSeat are injectable for testing. Summary: total/succeeded/failed/all_failed.

reuseCandidates.signature: function evaluateContext(signals, gaps, contradictions): { blockers, trace }
reuseCandidates.reuse: Convergence evaluator for context loops. Five signals (sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage, agreementRate, relevanceFloor, dependencyCompleteness). Three blocking guards.
reuseCandidates.notes: Reuse catalog coverage is weighted highest (0.30) per reuse-first principle. Slice coverage, relevance floor, and agreement rate are blocking guards.

reuseCandidates.signature: function evaluateResearch(signals, gaps, contradictions, unverified): { blockers, trace }
reuseCandidates.reuse: Convergence evaluator for research loops. Five signals (questionCoverage, claimVerificationRate, contradictionDensity, sourceDiversity, evidenceDepth) with blocking guards and weighted thresholds.
reuseCandidates.notes: Blocking guards for source_diversity and evidence_depth; weighted signals for question coverage and claim verification. Returns blockers[] + trace[].

reuseCandidates.signature: function evaluateReview(signals, gaps, contradictions): { blockers, trace }
reuseCandidates.reuse: Convergence evaluator for review loops. Five signals (dimensionCoverage, findingStability, p0ResolutionRate, evidenceDensity, hotspotSaturation). P0 resolution rate is blocking_guard.
reuseCandidates.notes: Dimension coverage and P0 resolution are blocking guards; finding stability and evidence density are weighted.

reuseCandidates.signature: function runLineageProcess(command, cmdArgs, opts): Promise<{ status, signal, stdout, error? }>
reuseCandidates.notes: Key design: returns Promise so the pool's K-in-flight cap actually overlaps. Drains stderr so child never blocks on full pipe. SIGTERM on timeout; signal field preserves timeout-as-failure signal.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function handleCodeGraphContext(args: ContextHandlerArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
reuseCandidates.notes: LLM-oriented graph neighborhoods. Delegates to buildContext() from code-graph-context.ts. Blocked-read path with canonical readiness vocabulary.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function handleCodeGraphScan(args: ScanArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }>
reuseCandidates.notes: Full/incremental scan: indexFiles → persistIndexedFileResult → cross-file edge resolution → dangling prune. Scope-change and zero-node guards. Reuse: trigger programmatic reindex.

reuseCandidates.signature: function queryEdgesFrom(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeTargetResult[]
reuseCandidates.notes: Outbound edges with optional edge-type filter. Returns {edge, targetNode}. Core building block for call-graph and import queries.

reuseCandidates.signature: function queryOutline(filePath: string): CodeNode[]
reuseCandidates.notes: Returns all code_nodes for a file. Used by query handler outline op and detect_changes. Direct DB query — no MCP wrapper needed.

reuseCandidates.signature: const CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS: ToolDefinition[]
reuseCandidates.notes: Array of 8 MCP tool definitions (scan/query/status/context/classify/verify/apply/detect_changes). Published to ListTools. Reuse: import to register same tools on a different server surface.

reuseCandidates.signature: function getEmbedderAdapter(provider: string, model: string, dimensionsOverride?: number): ExecutionRouterAdapter
reuseCandidates.notes: Returns cached or new ExecutionRouterAdapter for provider:model. Handles ollama delegation and factory-backed providers (openai/voyage/hf-local). Credential cache invalidation on adapter rotation.

reuseCandidates.signature: function startReindex(options: StartReindexOptions, runtimeOptions?: ReindexRuntimeOptions): string
reuseCandidates.notes: Queues embedder reindex job: reads memory_index rows, embeds via adapter, writes vectors to staging shard, atomic rename on completion. Returns job ID.

reuseCandidates.signature: Level 3 research coordination root: 88 findings, 10 ranked recommendations, trust-axis separation, honest measurement contract
reuseCandidates.notes: Key research outputs: research/research.md (canonical synthesis), research/recommendations.md (ranked decision surface), research/findings-registry.json (88 findings). The trust-axis separation (provenance, evidence status, freshness as separate fields) is a durable architectural decision (ADR R10 replacement).

reuseCandidates.signature: Skill-advisor graph, scoring engine, routing engine with playbook-run remediation
reuseCandidates.notes: 002-skill-advisor is in-progress. The advisor graph, scoring, and routing are live. Depends on 002 (internals) being stable before 006 (operator tooling) can reference it per phase handoff rules.

reuseCandidates.signature: MCP daemon lifecycle: IPC socket canonicalization, WAL checkpoint, graceful shutdown, provider dispose, watchdog, bridge liveness
reuseCandidates.notes: 7 sub-phases. Phases 1,3,4 complete (socket dir, research, nondestructive build). Phases 5-7 planned (provider dispose, watchdog, bridge liveness). The daemon-reliability research (003) produced a ranked, adversarially-verified fix roadmap.

reuseCandidates.signature: function createModelServerSupervisor(options?): ModelServerSupervisor
reuseCandidates.notes: Extracted to shared lib so both mk-spec-memory-launcher and mk-skill-advisor-launcher reuse it. Configurable via env: HF_MODEL_SERVER_*, WATCHDOG_*, CRASH_LOOP_*. The launcher-local factory pre-seeds writeLease/shouldExitLauncher/onRssBreach defaults.

reuseCandidates.signature: function computeCompositeScore(signals, loopType): number
reuseCandidates.reuse: Weighted composite convergence score computation. Different weight profiles for research (5 signals), review (5 signals), and context (5 signals). Returns 0-1 rounded to 3 decimals.
reuseCandidates.notes: Loop-type-specific weight tables; context weights reuseCatalog highest at 0.30. Used by convergence entrypoint to surface score alongside blockers.

reuseCandidates.signature: function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData): mergedRegistry
reuseCandidates.notes: Cross-model attribution via lineage labels. Computes avg convergence score across lineages. Exported for unit testing.

reuseCandidates.signature: function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData): mergedRegistry
reuseCandidates.notes: SEVERITY_RANK: P0=3, P1=2, P2=1. Cross-lineage P0 wins. Verdict: FAIL if any P0, CONDITIONAL if any P1, else PASS. Exported for unit testing.

reuseCandidates.signature: function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry): void
reuseCandidates.reuse: JSONL orchestration-status.log ledger appender. One line per lifecycle event (started/completed/failed/salvaged/converged).
reuseCandidates.notes: Simple fs.appendFileSync; follows the proven orchestration ledger pattern used across fan-out runs.

reuseCandidates.signature: function runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout): { salvaged, failed }
reuseCandidates.notes: Runs before the failure throw so iteration recovery is never lost. extractTextFromOpencodeJson parses opencode --format json text parts from stdout.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function handleDetectChanges(args: DetectChangesArgs): Promise<MCPResponse>
reuseCandidates.notes: Read-only preflight: maps unified-diff to structural symbols via line-range overlap. Blocks on stale graph. Reuse: integrate into CI/PR pipelines for impact analysis.

reuseCandidates.signature: function getStats(): { totalFiles, totalNodes, totalEdges, lastScanTimestamp, lastGitHead, dbFileSize, schemaVersion, nodesByKind, edgesByType, ... }
reuseCandidates.notes: Comprehensive graph health stats. Used by status handler. Returns DB file size, schema version, node/edge breakdowns, parse health summary.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function startIpcSocketServer(options: IpcSocketServerOptions): Promise<IpcSocketServerHandle>
reuseCandidates.notes: Multi-client IPC bridge over Unix socket. Each client gets its own MCP Server instance. Shared by memory/code-index/skill-advisor daemons. Reuse for new daemon service.

reuseCandidates.signature: function buildReadinessBlock(ready: ReadyResult): CodeGraphReadinessBlock
reuseCandidates.notes: Maps ReadyResult to CodeGraphReadinessBlock with canonicalReadiness + trustState. All 4 handlers emit this. Reuse: consistent readiness envelope for any new handler.

reuseCandidates.signature: function validateToolArgs(toolName: string, rawInput: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown>
reuseCandidates.notes: Validates MCP input against published schema: additionalProperties:false, enum, minLength. Throws field-specific Error. Reuse in any MCP dispatch layer.

reuseCandidates.signature: function getActiveEmbedder(db: Database.Database): ActiveEmbedder
reuseCandidates.notes: Reads active embedder name/dim/provider from vec_metadata table. Returns DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER when unset. Used by reindex and reconcile.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function generateDocumentEmbedding(text: string): Promise<Float32Array | null>
reuseCandidates.notes: Generates document embedding via lazy singleton provider. Circuit breaker: returns null on consecutive failures. LRU cache. Rate limiting. Primary embedding entry point for indexing.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function generateQueryEmbedding(query: string): Promise<Float32Array | null>
reuseCandidates.notes: Query embedding with lower cache priority than documents. Circuit breaker. Use for search query vectorization.

reuseCandidates.signature: Causal-graph channel routing MVP — 85% complete
reuseCandidates.notes: 002-causal-graph-channel-routing in-progress at 85%. The causal-graph channel is a distinct routing surface in the memory search pipeline.

reuseCandidates.signature: Doctor command surface with dependency-safe rebuild orchestration — 90% complete
reuseCandidates.notes: 002-doctor-update-orchestrator at 90%. The /doctor surface is the operator diagnostic entry point. Relocated doctor-install-alignment from skilled-agent-orchestration (context-index.md wave 8).

reuseCandidates.signature: function persistIndexedFileResult(result: ParseResult, opts?: { deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean }): void
reuseCandidates.notes: Atomic per-file persistence: upsertFile → replaceNodes → replaceEdges. Transactional. Used by scan handler loop.

reuseCandidates.signature: function resolveDimensions(provider: string, model: string, dimensions?: number): number
reuseCandidates.notes: Resolves embedding dimensions: explicit override > startup profile match > default. Used by getEmbedderAdapter and reindex. Import for consistent dim resolution.

reuseCandidates.signature: function ensureVecTableForDim(db: Database.Database, dim: number): string
reuseCandidates.notes: Creates vec_<dim> table if missing. Returns table name. Used by reindex before writing vectors. Idempotent.

reuseCandidates.signature: function invalidateProviderSingleton(): void
reuseCandidates.notes: Drops cached provider singleton so next getProvider() re-resolves. Call after active-embedder pointer changes (e.g. completed reindex). Prevents stale model/dim usage.

reuseCandidates.signature: Local-first resolveProvider() cascade (ADR-014) relocated from skilled-agent-orchestration
reuseCandidates.notes: 016-embedding-provider-local-first implements ADR-014. Sits with the embedding cluster (008/009/010/011). Key decision: local-first embedding provider cascade to avoid external dependency.

reuseCandidates.signature: Runtime hook parity across Claude, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode — 85% complete
reuseCandidates.notes: 001-hook-parity at 85%. Cross-runtime hook schema and wiring is the deliverable. Depends on 002-spec-kit-internals (advisor + template) being stable first.

reuseCandidates.signature: Codex hooks rewired from Claude scripts to purpose-built Codex adapters; PreCompact removed as unsupported
reuseCandidates.notes: Codex hooks now emit Codex-shaped envelopes. PreCompact hook removed — the hook_system.md contract lists Codex compaction as unsupported. Applied and smoke-verified.

reuseCandidates.signature: function resolveIpcSocketPath(dbDir: string): string
reuseCandidates.notes: Resolves daemon-ipc.sock path under the DB dir. Convention: <dbDir>/daemon-ipc.sock. Import for consistent socket path resolution.

reuseCandidates.signature: async function teardownEmbedderAfterSwap(provider: string, model: string): Promise<void>
reuseCandidates.notes: Disposes cached direct adapter after active-embedder swap. Called by reindex completion. Ensures stale provider resources are released.

reuseCandidates.signature: function cancelJob(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null
reuseCandidates.notes: Sets job status to 'cancelled'. Worker checks status before each batch and aborts cleanly. Staging shard is cleaned up.

reuseCandidates.signature: Verify-first scouted bugfix batches (3 completed) with release-readiness deep-review train
reuseCandidates.notes: The scouted-bugfix-train pattern (010-scouted-bugfix-train/ with batches 001-003) is a reusable verify-first remediation approach. Wave 8 relocated batches 1-3 from skilled-agent-orchestration. Batch-4 is the next slot.

reuseCandidates.signature: class BoundedMap<K, V> extends Map<K, V> { readonly maxSize: number }
reuseCandidates.notes: LRU-evicting Map with maxSize cap. Access promotes entry to most-recent. Reuse for any bounded in-memory cache.

reuseCandidates.signature: class TtlMap<K, V> { set(key, value, ttlMs?), get(key), pruneExpired() }
reuseCandidates.notes: TTL + LRU bounded cache. BoundedMap-backed. Auto-prunes expired entries on access. Reuse for time-sensitive caches.

reuseCandidates.signature: function rotateIfNeeded(auditPath: string, maxBytes: number, maxFiles: number, now?: Date): boolean
reuseCandidates.notes: Rotates audit log when exceeding maxBytes, enforces maxFiles cap. Reuse for any log rotation need.

integrationPoints.signature: async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder({ serviceName, leaseResult, loggerPrefix, dbDir, legacyReport?, probeTimeoutMs?, connect?, bridge? }): { action, reason, socketPath? }
integrationPoints.notes: deepProbe=true detects hung daemon (accepts connection but never responds). Prefers stored lease socketPath over recomputation. Speckit IPC supports both UDS and tcp:// endpoints. Used by all 3 launcher types.

integrationPoints.signature: function createSessionProxy({ socketPath, stdin?, stdout?, probe?, connect?, log?, classify?, maxReattachAttempts?, maxColdStartAttempts?, keepaliveIntervalMs? }): { start, stop }
integrationPoints.notes: Three states: REATTACHING → CONNECTED → CLOSED. Deep-probe wait with backoff ladder [100,250,500,1000,1500]ms. Pending request tracker distinguishes replayable (memory_search, session_resume, etc) from unsafe (memory_delete, checkpoint_restore). Keepalive pings every 10s with 5s timeout. Protocol version mismatch is terminal (non-retryable -32002).

integrationPoints.signature: function acquireOwnerLeaseFile(): { acquired, lease?, holder?, classification, reclaimed? }
integrationPoints.notes: Heartbeat TTL: 60s, refreshed at TTL/2 interval. Re-read after atomic rename confirms surviving owner. classifyOwnerLease checks liveness + ppid + heartbeat. writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive uses wx+fsync for crash durability.
Returns singleton better-sqlite3 handle for code-graph.sqlite. All DB operations flow through this. Lazy-init via initDb(). Central seam for DB access.

integrationPoints.signature: async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null>
integrationPoints.notes: Central dispatch: validates args via validateToolArgs(), routes to 8 handler functions. Returns null for unknown tools. Composable — wrap or extend for new tool surfaces.

integrationPoints.signature: function scoreAdvisorPrompt(prompt, options): { recommendations, ambiguous, unknown }
integrationPoints.notes: Lane weights overridable via SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON env. Class C breadth-build and multi-concern abstention gates prevent routing broad greenfield builds to single skills. Shadow scoring with learned weights runs alongside live scoring for delta telemetry.
Three service names with distinct default DB dirs. tcp:// endpoints bypass file-existence checks. Socket file name: daemon-ipc.sock. Used by all launcher types for bridge socket resolution.

integrationPoints.signature: function probeDaemon(socketPath, options?): Promise<{ status, reason }>
integrationPoints.notes: Uses StringDecoder for multibyte UTF-8 safety across chunk boundaries. Bounded probe timeout (default 5s, max 6999ms). The reap/bridge decision always passes deepProbe:true.

Deps are injectable for testing (getContextChild, hfControl, isChildRunning, etc). Key design: lease is NOT cleared during recycle — launcher process stays alive, replacement daemon respawns via backoff. rssBreachSelfExitInProgress flag prevents re-entrant recycle.

integrationPoints.signature: YAML workflow: flock → probe → migrate → snapshot → dashboard → dependency.order → validation → state.log
integrationPoints.notes: SIGINT follows ADR-001/ADR-007: set cancel, ~5s SQLite settle window, restore in-flight snapshot, log, exit 130. Every abort path must rm -f .doctor-update.flock before exit. State log at mcp_server/database/.doctor-update.last-run.json.

Writer lock path: .deep-loop-graph-writer.lock or .council-graph-writer.lock. Events input (--events) filters into nodes/edges by type field. VALID_KINDS and VALID_RELATIONS are per-loop-type whitelists from the DB module.

Creates MCP Server with ListTools + CallTool handlers dispatching via codeGraphTools.dispatch(). IPC bridge + idle monitor + owner-lease lifecycle wired here. A new daemon service copies this pattern.

integrationPoints.signature: async function handleAdvisorRecommend(args: unknown): Promise<{ content: [{ type: string, text: string }] }>
integrationPoints.notes: Prompt-safe: never persists raw prompt text. Cache key includes sourceSignature (freshness:generation:lastGenerationBump). SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1 returns disabledOutput. Default thresholds: 0.8 confidence, 0.35 uncertainty.

integrationPoints.signature: YAML workflow: Discovery → gate → analysis → gate → drift classification → recommendation → state log
integrationPoints.notes: Mutation boundaries explicitly listed: allowed_targets are provider-keyed sqlite DBs only. Forbidden: spec docs, skill files, code-graph DBs, deep-loop DBs. Rebuilds owned by /doctor:update.
Resolved code-graph DB directory. Skill-local: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database. Overridable via SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR. All DB paths derive from this.

integrationPoints.signature: async function dispatch(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse>
integrationPoints.notes: Thin wrapper over handleTool with unknown-tool error fallback. Called by index.ts CallToolRequestSchema handler.

conventions.signature: function validateNamespaceValue(value, key, makeInputError): string
conventions.notes: findRepoRoot walks up looking for .git marker. isInsideRepo checks relative path doesn't escape. Defense-in-depth against path traversal in namespace-scoped values.

conventions.signature: Memory store and code graph are distinct structural surfaces that cross-link, not a shared store
conventions.notes: Architectural boundary: memory (003) and code-graph (004) are separate subsystems. Phase handoff rule spec.md:120 states 'code-graph is a distinct structural surface that cross-links to memory, not a shared store'. Any future work must respect this split.

conventions.signature: Level 1/2/3/3+ is the SOLE public/AI-facing vocabulary; preset/capability/kind are STRICTLY private
conventions.notes: AI behavior + user conversation flow must remain byte-identical. Workflow-invariance CI test greps public surfaces for banned vocabulary. Internal vocabulary lives ONLY in manifest + resolver + shell wrapper. Any new feature that would require exposing preset/capability to users must add a new Level instead.

conventions.signature: Bridge fix reverted: runnable bridge initializes memory DB directly in second process — exact corruption class the single-writer lease prevents
conventions.notes: Broken-and-inert is safer than working-and-racy. The bridge stays inert until 028 code-index phase delivers IPC-backed transport. Any future code that touches the memory DB from outside the daemon lease MUST go through IPC, never direct init.

conventions.signature: function classifyFrame(frame): boolean
conventions.notes: initialize and ping are always replayable. notifications/ methods are replayable. tools/call checks tool name against two sets. memory_save is replayable due to content-hash dedup.

conventions.signature: function writeAtomicJsonFile(targetPath, payload, operation): boolean
conventions.notes: Convention across launcher subsystem: write tmp → rename atomic. Durable-write failures are logged once and return false (best-effort) rather than crashing. Used for lease files, pid files, state files.

conventions.signature: function acquireWriterLock(lockPath): () => void (release)
conventions.notes: Retry interval defaults 25ms, max wait 1s, configurable via DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS env. Atomics.wait-based sleepSync for contention backoff.

conventions.signature: Public preserves its split semantic/structural/continuity topology; seam-hardening contracts precede packaging
conventions.notes: The v2 headline decision: do NOT replace Public's split topology with a monolithic facade. Front-load trust and freshness contracts before any packaging work. This is NFR-T01 (topology preservation).

conventions.signature: mk-code-graph bridge restore REVERTED due to dual-writer hazard on memory DB; fix deferred to IPC-backed transport in 028
conventions.notes: Critical safety decision: the bridge import fix was attempted and reverted because the bridge's startup path calls initializeDb()+sessionManager.init() against the memory DB the daemon owns — the incident the 028 research forbids. Proper fix is IPC-backed transport (028 code-index phase 3).

conventions.signature: Migration bridge resolving 17 top-level phases to 7 themed parents with old-path resolution tables
conventions.notes: Old phase paths (000-016) are NOT rewritten; resolve via bridge tables. Any new code or docs referencing old paths must consult this file. Wave 8 (2026-06-03) relocated 6 skilled-agent-orchestration packets into 026 tracks.

conventions.signature: PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 is the controlling license for external-project patterns; the actual shipped mitigation was an upstream-project name scrub (commit bdd3c831d) not a verbatim license quote; P0 governance gate blocking all 012 code sub-phases; .gitignore excludes external/ so worktrees lack the LICENSE file
conventions.reuse: Any future code touching external-project patterns must honor the clean-room allow-list and name-scrub posture documented in the ADR; verbatim-code reuse remains forbidden.
conventions.notes: The spec's status-note documents a material change in mitigation strategy (name scrub vs verbatim quote) that the decision-record ADR captures. A planner must know the current posture, not the historical one.

conventions.signature: Old structural-artifact bundle replaced by trust-axis separation: provenance, evidence status, freshness authority as separate fields
conventions.notes: Contract before packaging: any downstream reader trying to treat cached startup, dashboard, or structural artifacts as ready-made defaults must be pointed back to trust-axis and freshness prerequisites first. R10 replacement is explicit in spec and ADRs.

conventions.signature: C+F hybrid manifest-driven greenfield: 86→15 source files; levels eliminated internally; single manifest drives scaffold+validator
conventions.notes: Three orthogonal axes replace level scalar: kind + capabilities + presets. Single manifest (spec-kit-docs.json) drives scaffolder AND validator — drift structurally impossible. Inline section gates preserve markdown authoring. Follow-on packet implements this; 026 only designs it.

conventions.signature: Single index-scope.ts is the canonical source for memory/code-graph path exclusions; spec-doc filters are additive overlays only
conventions.notes: Changing EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY automatically changes spec-doc classification and discovery. z_archive is intentionally NOT excluded (ARCHIVE_MULTIPLIERS 0.1 decay handles deprioritization). Any new exclusion must land in index-scope.ts, not scattered walkers.

conventions.signature: Code graph excludes .opencode/skills/** by default; 97% files/98.3% nodes were skill internals
conventions.notes: Env SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true or per-call includeSkills:true for opt-in. Per-call overrides env (ADR-002). Scope fingerprint stored in code_graph_metadata; requires full scan when stored and active scope differ. Any scan-default change must update the fingerprint and require explicit full scan.

conventions.signature: Standalone system_code_graph MCP registration; ADR-001 Q3 (co-resident) explicitly superseded
conventions.notes: Namespace changes from mcp__mk_spec_memory__code_graph_* to mcp__system_code_graph__*. Tool IDs remain stable. Path-flattening canonical: no mcp_server/code_graph/ intermediate. Any new MCP server follows this standalone topology pattern.

conventions.signature: Lease PID accepted as owner only if alive AND real start time matches lease-recorded start time within 2s
conventions.notes: Closes PID-recycling false-SKIP that masks crashes as healthy. Uses `ps -o lstart=`; falls back to liveness-only on unreadable start time. Any daemon lease validation must include start-time identity check.

conventions.signature: Three-way isolation: system-code-graph extraction as standalone MCP, distinct from spec-kit-memory and spec-kit-shared
conventions.notes: 006-extraction-and-isolation completed the standalone-MCP pivot. The three-way isolation (memory / code-graph / shared) is an architectural boundary to respect.

conventions.signature: Divergent signal-handler stacks (context-server.ts:1681-1692 exit 0 vs shutdown-hooks.ts:129-148 exit 143/130) race under concurrent-session SIGTERM; if shutdown-hooks stack wins it skips fatalShutdown ordered drain, coupling unfenced ingest worker dirty-WAL with non-re--entrant socket server; operationally reachable with 2 Claude + 4 codex sessions sharing one repo
conventions.reuse: This compound failure analysis is the justification for the single-ordered-teardown fix in 017-last-50-commits-review-remediation; any future shutdown-path change must preserve the ordering.
conventions.notes: The 017 remediation landed a single ordered teardown, but the compound-failure analysis documents exactly which code paths interact and why ordering matters. A planner modifying shutdown hooks must read this.

conventions.signature: function classifyExitCode(err): number
conventions.notes: Convention: 0=ok, 1=script error, 2=DB error, 3=input validation error. Every CLI entrypoint follows this contract.

conventions.signature: const SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY: readonly { id, weight, shadowWeight, live, damping? }[]
conventions.notes: isLiveScorerLane() gate excludes non-live lanes from scoring. Shadow weights mirror live weights for A/B telemetry. DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS exported for shadow-only scoring paths.

conventions.signature: Publish provisional honest measurement contract before any multiplier claims
conventions.notes: Decision: no performance claims without the measurement contract. This gates downstream packaging work (including 005 deferred track).

conventions.signature: Root-cause map: RC-1 OOM/embedding RSS, RC-2 no watchdog, RC-3 dead-socket bridge, RC-4 build wipes dist, RC-5 /tmp socket dir
conventions.notes: The RC taxonomy from the daemon-reliability research is the canonical failure-mode reference. Phases 5-7 map to RC-1 through RC-3. Phase 4 fixed RC-4. Phase 1 fixed RC-5.

conventions.signature: Eight specific defaults chosen for remaining P1 remediation: auto modes remove waits, /memory:* stays markdown-only, /memory:save plan-only-by-default, embedding-cache rows deleted on retention delete, memory_save planner inputs internal-only, governed-ingest validation extends to ingest paths, args validated before pre-dispatch logic, legacy strict-warning packet grandfathered
conventions.reuse: These defaults are release-gate constraints that any future planner must honor; they shape the memory_save MCP schema, /memory:* command architecture, and ingest governance contract.
conventions.notes: A /speckit:plan author touching memory_save, /memory:*, or ingest governance needs these defaults to avoid re-litigating decisions already made under release-readiness pressure.

conventions.signature: /memory:save defaults to planner-first output (non-mutating); full-auto is explicit fallback only
conventions.notes: Default path returns structured planner output instead of mutating. Reconsolidation-on-save (ADR-003) and post-insert enrichment (ADR-004) are opt-in only. Quality-loop auto-fix (ADR-006) removed from default path. Hard legality checks remain as blockers.

conventions.signature: Delete all z_future rows regardless of tier rather than downgrading; z_future must never be indexed
conventions.notes: The invariant is 'z_future must never be indexed' — not 'lower priority'. Downgrading leaves forbidden rows searchable. Constitutional auto-surface was dominated by z_future research chunks before cleanup.

conventions.signature: Invalid constitutional saves downgraded to important with governance_audit row; path must contain /constitutional/
conventions.notes: Constitutional tier is path-gated: only files under /constitutional/ can be constitutional. Enforced at SQL write layer (ADR-007), not just handler layer. Any mutation surface (save, update, checkpoint restore, post-insert) must enforce this guard.

conventions.signature: Constitutional-tier guard enforced at SQL write layer; checkpoint restore validates rows inside transaction barrier
conventions.notes: Handler-only checks leave blind spots for internal callers and restore paths. Restore rejection is all-or-nothing (atomic). Audit uses action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'. Any new mutation surface must go through the SQL-layer guard.

conventions.signature: resolveCanonicalPath() prefers fs.realpathSync() to prevent symlinks from bypassing invariant enforcement
conventions.notes: Symlinked files can bypass path-based exclusion checks if only path.resolve() is used. Falls back to caller-supplied path for missing/broken targets so atomic save flows still work.

conventions.signature: Model-keyed PREFIX_REGISTRY + env-var override replacing hardcoded Nomic prefix; empty string = no prefix
conventions.notes: Two registries to keep in sync (TS HfLocalProvider + Python cocoindex). Legacy TASK_PREFIX export retained for back-compat. Empty string is a valid override (explicitly no prefix); undefined means fall through to registry. Wrong prefix silently degrades retrieval ~5-8%.

conventions.signature: Two-step pipeline: generator scaffolds structure and output path, Sonnet agent rewrites Summary/Added/Changed/Fixed/Files-Changed/Prune-FollowUps using a written contract; 441 phases need HVR-voice changelogs; fabrication guard plus adversarial verify; flat-per-track layout with 696 final files (624 leaf + 72 rollups + 2 indexes)
conventions.reuse: The changelog generator at scripts/changelog/ and the enrichment contract are reusable infrastructure for any future packet-local changelog generation.
conventions.notes: Operationally important: the root rollup index files in tracks 002/006/007 were NOT refreshed after the flat-per-track flatten (review-changelog-completeness/review-report.md:95). They still link to old nested directory shapes and under-report. A regeneration pass is needed.

conventions.signature: Cleanup apply rebuilds its plan inside the transaction instead of trusting pre-transaction snapshot
conventions.notes: Concurrent writes between plan generation and apply could leave violating rows behind. Dry-run output remains informative but the authoritative plan for mutation lives inside the transaction. Any cleanup/repair CLI must rebuild its plan inside the apply transaction.

conventions.signature: SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION parsed as permissive boolean; '0'/'no'/'off'/'' now correctly disable
conventions.notes: BREAKING for users setting =0 (they now actually get disabled). Default-ON preserved. Aligns with how every other SPECKIT_* flag is parsed. Any new SPECKIT_* env flag must use the same permissive boolean parser.

conventions.signature: ONNX Runtime backend rejected: 5x slower on Apple Silicon at parity-preserving settings; sbert remains default
conventions.notes: Neural Engine barely active for Gemma (23% ops, 77% CPU). Static-pad128 is fast but breaks parity (mean cosine 0.869). At parity-preserving settings ONNX is 3.5x slower. The ONNX code path is removed; rejected-experiment record preserved in packet.

conventions.signature: Reddit post treated as primary-source field report with appropriate skepticism; findings passage-anchored
conventions.notes: Every finding anchors to specific paragraph. Recommendation labels reflect evidence quality, not assumed validity. Structural conclusions stand independently of exact percentages. Any future research phase must preserve this source-framing discipline.

conventions.signature: 926-vs-858 sessions and 18903-vs-11357 turns discrepancies preserved explicitly, never normalized
conventions.notes: Smoothing introduces artificial precision the source doesn't support. NFR-D01 in spec.md makes preservation a non-functional requirement. Downstream planning can bound precision correctly. Any document referencing these counts must show both numbers.

conventions.signature: content-router.ts has documented scoped preservation exception for Tier 3 default-disable guard
conventions.notes: Eight-category switch, Tier 1 logic, Tier 2 matching, and target selection are preserved. The documented exception is the Tier 3 default-disable and manual-review guard. Changelog and release notes must reflect this honestly.

dependencies.signature: invalidateEntityDensityCache() wired into post-commit branch of memory_save and memory_bulk_delete handlers
dependencies.notes: Call AFTER commit, not before — failed saves must NOT invalidate cache. Per-call overhead ~µs (in-process Map clear). Burst writes now propagate to entity-density signal immediately. Any handler that mutates memory_index rows must invalidate the cache post-commit.

dependencies.signature: Local-embedding foundation with ollama/bge promotion and rerank-sidecar — 75% complete
dependencies.notes: 003-embedder-testing-and-architecture at 75%. Embedding consolidation (010-embedding-consolidation-hf-local-server) and stack hardening (011-embedding-stack-hardening) are in-progress children. The local-first resolveProvider() cascade (ADR-014) is the key decision.

dependencies.signature: Code graph upgrades depend on packet 011 (validator owner) as hard predecessor; additive enrichment only
dependencies.notes: 011 owns fail-closed trust validation. 014 adds edge evidence classes, confidence backfill, detector provenance only after 011 stabilizes. Routing facade, Leiden clustering, GraphML/Cypher exports are prototype-later (ADR-003). Any graph enrichment must not compete with 011's trust-axis ownership.

gaps.signature: Deferred adoption-uplift track (phase runner, edge explanation, affordance evidence, causal-trust display)
gaps.notes: 005 is the only deferred top-level track (5% complete). Its children (001-phase-runner, 002-edge-explanation, 003-affordance-evidence, 004-causal-trust-display) are all deferred. Two children (005-deep-review-findings, 006-deep-research-review) are abandoned. This is the primary gap in the 026 program.

gaps.signature: getGraphReadinessSnapshot and getGraphFreshness are documented/contractual read-only but call detectState which writes last_git_head to DB, violating ADR-003 single-writer discipline; function docstrings and status.ts inline comment claim no side effects; P1 idempotent non-corrupting
gaps.reuse: Fix: move setLastGitHead out of detectState into ensureCodeGraphReady only, or pass mutate:false. The proposed fix is sound and documented.
gaps.notes: A /speckit:plan author touching code-graph status/readiness must know this contract violation exists. The write is idempotent (SQLite serializes, same value) so it's non-corrupting, but it makes the read-only promise false.

gaps.signature: OpenCode code-graph plugin bridge (mk-code-graph-bridge.mjs) is non-functional by deliberate choice; import fix was attempted, smoke-verified, then REVERTED because the runnable bridge calls initializeDb()/sessionManager.init() directly against daemon-owned memory DB (dual-writer hazard); root fix deferred to IPC-backed transport in 028 code-index runtime-integration phase
gaps.reuse: The bridge stays inert; the README documents the non-functional state. Any planner must know the OpenCode code-graph integration path requires 028 to ship first.
gaps.notes: This is a genuine open gap: the OpenCode code-graph integration is blocked on 028's IPC-backed transport. The bridge's dual-writer hazard was caught by fresh-model review after mechanical verification passed.

gaps.signature: Per-track ROOT ROLLUP index files not refreshed after 026 changelog tree was flattened to one-subdir-level / flat-files-per-track; they still link to old nested directory shapes and stop at pre-flatten dates, under-reporting and mis-linking the (complete, present) leaf changelogs
gaps.reuse: A single regeneration pass over the *-root.md rollups in tracks 002/006/007 would clear this drift.
gaps.notes: Concrete documentation gap: the leaf layer is healthy but the index layer drifted. An operator reading the root rollups sees incomplete coverage.

gaps.signature: Orphan launcher sweep was verified no-op: all 9 launcher parents are live sessions; launcher-lifecycle fix deferred to 028
gaps.notes: Launchers ARE those sessions' MCP transports — killing them breaks working sessions. The real launcher-lifecycle fix for true owner-exit orphans stays with 028 skill-advisor workstream. Do not attempt to kill old launchers without parent-process classification.

gaps.signature: daemon-recycle-transparency-stress.vitest.ts uses a local replay loop copy rather than the production replaySnapshot() closure in launcher-session-proxy.cjs:595; cannot catch drift in the production closure
gaps.reuse: The stress test should drive an exported production replay helper or a real proxy recycle path; currently it validates the classifyFrame logic but not the production closure.
gaps.notes: P1 test-fidelity finding from the 014 deep review. Downgrade only if the test drives an exported production replay helper.

gaps.signature: Concrete-token spec-folder naming policy — deferred at 0%
gaps.notes: Deferred child: naming policy for spec folders. No work started. May matter if future program packets need consistent naming.

gaps.signature: Install guides/scripts + doctor realignment for post-CocoIndex world — deferred
gaps.notes: Deferred at 0%. Post-CocoIndex install scripts not yet updated. Blocked by CocoIndex decoupling (004-code-graph/002) being finalized.

lowConfidence.signature: Clean-room license audit for external-project adoption — deferred
lowConfidence.notes: Deferred child. Blocks safe external-project adoption licensing. Low urgency until adoption track (005) is prioritized.
